title: DDoS Attack
description: Overwhelming a target with traffic from thousands of sources to make it unavailable.
fullExplanation: A Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attack uses thousands of compromised machines (botnet) to flood a target with traffic, exhausting bandwidth, CPU, or connection tables. Unlike DoS (single source), DDoS is distributed — blocking one IP doesn't help. Attacks range from volumetric (flood bandwidth) to protocol (exhaust state tables) to application layer (HTTP floods). Largest recorded: 3.47 Tbps (Microsoft Azure, 2021).

types.name: Volumetric
types.description: Flood bandwidth with UDP/ICMP traffic. Amplification attacks multiply traffic 100x+.

types.name: Protocol / State Exhaustion
types.description: SYN flood, ACK flood — exhaust firewall/server connection state tables.

types.name: Application Layer (L7)
types.description: HTTP GET/POST floods — mimic legitimate traffic, hard to distinguish.

types.name: Amplification
types.description: Spoof victim's IP, send small request to open resolver/NTP — large response floods victim.

types.name: Slowloris
types.description: Open many HTTP connections, send headers slowly — exhaust server thread pool.

keyFacts: Largest DDoS: 3.47 Tbps against Microsoft Azure (2021)
Mirai botnet (2016): 600 Gbps — used IoT devices (cameras, routers)
Cloudflare, Akamai, AWS Shield absorb DDoS at the edge
BGP anycast: route attack traffic to scrubbing centers worldwide
Application layer DDoS hardest to mitigate — looks like real traffic

defenses.name: CDN / DDoS Scrubbing
defenses.description: Cloudflare, Akamai, AWS Shield absorb volumetric attacks at edge

defenses.name: Rate Limiting
defenses.description: Limit requests per IP per second at network/application layer

defenses.name: Anycast Diffusion
defenses.description: Distribute traffic across global PoPs — dilute attack volume

defenses.name: BCP38 / Ingress Filtering
defenses.description: ISPs drop spoofed source IPs — prevents amplification attacks

defenses.name: SYN Cookies
defenses.description: Eliminate SYN flood vulnerability in TCP stack

defenses.name: CAPTCHA / JS Challenge
defenses.description: Cloudflare Under Attack Mode — verify humans before serving
